model.quantization: Float16 model and Metal compute; Float32 CPU activations and mel filter
model.license: MIT source model and official converter; pinned converted model card has no structured license field

runtime.boundary: Rust f32 PCM and owned JSON through a repository-owned task-level C ABI to the official MLX C++ core

energy.notes: No alternating powermetrics capture was made. Relative energy remains part of the broader #4 bakeoff, not the #15 task-boundary proof.

engineering_cost.conversion_effort: The official MLX Examples converter produced the pinned complete NPZ. The repository fetcher verifies and extracts all 166 model tensors, mel filter, and multilingual tokenizer; product inference contains no Python or Node logic.
engineering_cost.update_cost: The repository owns a model-specific C++ task adapter over pinned official MLX. The earlier v0.31.2 to v0.32.0 control required no source edits, but model graph and runtime upgrades remain explicit retest events.
engineering_cost.packaging_cost: The proof needs a 18,675,320-byte shim, a colocated 130,164,152-byte metallib, an executable-relative RPATH, and the 74,418,182-byte model NPZ. Kernel and artifact pruning remain productization work.

observations: The Rust-owned task path produced the same complete transcript, tokens, and two segment boundaries in six CPU and six GPU runs.
The transcript differed from the fixture reference only by pluralizing the final word.
The text matched the pinned official mlx-whisper 0.4.3 reference; the final boundary was 100 ms later under full-context decoder recomputation.
The recorded cold-load value is process-cold but not a clean-host claim because system and Metal caches were not cleared.
Cancellation between decoder steps, long-audio chunking, streaming updates, KV caching, and artifact pruning are bounded follow-up work rather than unresolved ABI feasibility.
